findings.claim: ADRs 0040, 0041, 0042, and 0043 form a chain of four Accepted records that all circle the same unresolved primitive — no build-time or placement-time measure can yet discriminate a deliberate connector bridge/overlap from an actual body weld — and none of them closes that discrimination problem; each parks, banks, or re-locates it instead.

citations.quote:
no build-time
measure can tell that intentional overlap from a body weld

the
same root as ADR 0040: no build-time measure separates the connector bridge from
a weld.

BANK thin-hand
join evenness at ~B and defer it to a focused milestone.

That discrimination is ADR
0040's assembled-glyph problem in its minimal, tractable form — the geometry is now
known at placement time.

findings.consequence: A maintainer should treat this as a single open decision with a four-record paper trail rather than four separate closed decisions, and should track the bridge-vs-weld discrimination layer as the one blocking milestone before thin-hand/flashy-hand join evenness can ship.
